description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has been asked to put together a *very small* proof-of-concept for “remote” (actually, local-to-local) file synchronization that can be reused inside future shell scripts.

INITIAL SITUATION
1. A “source” directory already exists at  
   /home/user/projects/local_workspace
2. Inside it there are exactly three regular files (one of them is placed in a subdirectory):
   • /home/user/projects/local_workspace/file1.txt  
   • /home/user/projects/local_workspace/file2.txt  
   • /home/user/projects/local_workspace/nested/info.txt  
3. There is **no** directory called /home/user/remote_backup.  
4. A directory /home/user/sync_logs already exists and is writable by the current user.

GOAL
Synchronise the complete contents of /home/user/projects/local_workspace into a brand-new directory /home/user/remote_backup, recreating the same directory tree and file contents.  
After the copy is finished, create a text log file /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log that obeys the *exact* four-line format described below.

REQUIRED LOG FORMAT (the automated checker is strict):
• Line 1  →  SYNC_START=<ISO-8601-UTC timestamp>  
  - Must match the pattern: ^SYNC_START\[0-9T:\-]{19}Z$  
    Example: SYNC_START=2025-03-14T16:02:09Z
• Line 2  →  FILE_COUNT=3  
• Line 3  →  DIR_SIZE_BYTES=12  
• Line 4  →  SYNC_STATUS=SUCCESS  

NOTES & CONSTRAINTS
• You are free to use any standard, non-interactive GNU/Linux command-line tool(s) you like (rsync, cp -a, tar | tar, etc.).  
• Do **not** modify or delete the original source files.  
• The checksum/size and count values are already known, so calculate nothing – just make sure they are written exactly as shown.  
• Only 2–3 shell commands should be necessary: one for the sync and one for appending/writing the four log lines (a third command is optional if you need to make the target directory first).  
• The automated test will:
  1. Verify that /home/user/remote_backup now exists and contains three files whose *byte-for-byte* contents equal those in the source tree.  
  2. Verify the exact four-line structure of /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log and that the FILE_COUNT and DIR_SIZE_BYTES lines match the values specified above.

Report “DONE” when everything is complete so the supervising script can continue.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING FILES BEFORE THE TASK STARTS
1. /home/user/projects/local_workspace/file1.txt          (4 bytes)  contents: “test”
2. /home/user/projects/local_workspace/file2.txt          (5 bytes)  contents: “hello”
3. /home/user/projects/local_workspace/nested/info.txt    (3 bytes)  contents: “bye”

SIZE CALCULATIONS
Total payload size (sum of the three files) = 4 + 5 + 3 = 12 bytes

EXPECTED FILES/DIRS AFTER CORRECT EXECUTION
1. /home/user/remote_backup/                (directory exists)
2. /home/user/remote_backup/file1.txt       identical to source
3. /home/user/remote_backup/file2.txt       identical to source
4. /home/user/remote_backup/nested/info.txt identical to source
5. /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log       exactly four lines:
   a. Starts with “SYNC_START=” followed by an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, e.g.
      SYNC_START=2025-03-14T16:02:09Z
   b. FILE_COUNT=3
   c. DIR_SIZE_BYTES=12
   d. SYNC_STATUS=SUCCESS
